message.content:
please you have to fix all montioned the security and other issues everything should be downloaded and run using docker no local running and this is the original prompt # Original Prompt â€” CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations Platform

A CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations platform capable of running entirely on a disconnected local network supports district staff in managing classroom operations data alongside on-premise logistics fulfillment for devices, materials, and lost-and-found returns. Primary roles include Administrator (system setup and security), Operations Manager (warehouses, carriers, and service policies), Classroom Supervisor (real-time monitoring and incident closure), Customer Service Agent (tickets and compensation), and Auditor (read-only review of sensitive actions). The Vue.js web console provides a real-time dashboard that highlights classroom online status, a recognition confidence indicator, and an anomaly event stream that operators can acknowledge, assign, and resolve with a required resolution note. A separate parking operations view shows available-space counts, turnover (entries/hour), and exception alerts such as no plate captured, overtime, unsettled session, duplicate plate, or inconsistent entry/exit; each alert must be closed within 15 minutes or escalates to a supervisor queue. Users can configure shipping fee templates by weight (lb), item count, region, and tier (e.g., a $6.95 base fee up to 2 lb plus $1.25 per additional lb, with Alaska/Hawaii surcharges), define delivery zones and non-serviceable ZIP codes, and maintain warehouses and carrier options. A standardized after-sales ticketing flow supports delay, dispute, and lost item cases with evidence upload (photo and text), automated compensation suggestions (e.g., $10.00 credit for delivery over 48 hours late, capped at $50.00 per ticket) plus manual approval, and SLA timers visible on the ticket timeline. Membership management allows tiered benefits and growth points, with member-only pricing and coupons applied at checkout-like fulfillment requests; stored value is optional and, if enabled, supports top-up and spending within the platform UI with clear balance feedback and printable receipts.

The backend uses Express to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend over the local network, with consistent idempotency keys for retries on create/update operations and a job monitor for background tasks such as bulk imports and shipment status sync. MySQL persists master data (students, classes, departments, courses, semesters) with uniqueness and referential integrity enforced, plus bulk import/export from Excel/CSV with row-level validation and an error report downloadable for correction. Logistics data models link shipment records to parcels and to after-sales tickets so a single parcel can be referenced during disputes; tracking numbers are stored and shipment-tracking "sync APIs" are implemented as internal connectors to on-prem carrier systems only, with no internet dependency. Observability is built into the admin console using locally collected runtime metrics (p95 latency, CPU/GPU utilization if present, and error rate), searchable application logs with retention of 30 days, and configurable thresholds that trigger on-screen alerts and optional local email-free notifications (e.g., banner and audible workstation alert). File handling uses local object storage on a mounted disk path, with image compression/cropping and basic content safeguards limited to format/size checks and perceptual hashing for duplicate detection; uploads are capped at 10 MB per file and only JPEG/PNG are accepted. Security requires username/password login with salted hashing, role-based access control, field-level masking for personally identifiable information, AES-256 encryption for stored value balances and audit logs at rest, TLS for transport even on LAN, API signing for privileged integrations, rate limiting and circuit breakers to prevent overload, and scheduled backups with tested restore procedures (daily full backup, 14-day retention) to a separate local disk.
 and then make sure everything works perfectly the ui the endpoints the test coverage should be more than 90 percent make the tests real specially the api tests and make them strong\
\

# TASK-15 Delivery Acceptance / Architecture Review (Static + Constraint-Bounded)

Date: 2026-03-31
Workspace: TASK-44/TASK-15
Inspector mode: Security-first, requirement-to-evidence acceptance audit

## 0) Ordered Checklist Execution

1. Runnability and prompt-baseline alignment - Completed
2. Architecture/module wiring review - Completed
3. Security-priority review (authn/authz/object/data isolation/admin-debug protection) - Completed
4. Mandatory static test coverage audit (unit + API mapping) - Completed
5. Prioritized issue consolidation and final judgment - Completed

## 1) Verification Boundary and Reproducibility

### Boundary constraints applied
- Per user instruction, Docker and related commands were not executed.
- This project is container-first and test runner is Docker-dependent:
  - TASK-15/repo/README.md:49-56
  - TASK-15/repo/run_tests.sh:33-39

### Confirmable vs unconfirmable scope
- Confirmed: static code/doc/test assertions, route wiring, middleware ordering, schema constraints, test intent and gaps.
- Unconfirmed (runtime only): end-to-end behavior under real DB/Redis/worker runtime, live websocket flows, backup job execution logs, restore execution outcomes.

## 2) Acceptance Verdict Matrix (Item-by-Item)

| Acceptance Area | Verdict | Evidence | Judgment Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Disconnected LAN operation, no internet dependency | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/README.md:3,198; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/shipment/carrier-sync.service.ts:20 | Declared LAN-only and carrier sync simulated locally. |
| Role model + RBAC matrix exists | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/backend/src/lib/permissions.ts:8-86,88-182 | Role/permission matrix is comprehensive and route permissions are broadly present. |
| Classroom operational dashboard capabilities | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/frontend/src/views/classroom/ClassroomOperationsView.vue:17-31,94-101,126-131,160-177 | Online status, confidence indicator, anomaly count, and live socket indicator are implemented. |
| Parking 15-minute alert SLA and escalation | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/backend/src/config/index.ts:89-91; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/parking/alert.service.ts:134,247-269; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/jobs/workers/parking-escalation.worker.ts:9-18 | SLA deadline and auto-escalation worker logic exist. |
| Logistics + fulfillment domain model linkage | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/backend/prisma/schema.prisma:575-636 | Shipment, parcel, and after-sales ticket relations are present. |
| Master data integrity constraints | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/backend/prisma/schema.prisma:93-94,107,120,150,158,160 | Uniqueness and referential constraints are present in schema. |
| File handling safeguards (mime/size/magic bytes/hash) | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/after-sales/evidence.service.ts:13-18,104-123,151-166 | JPEG/PNG checks, max size 10MB, magic-byte checks, compression, duplicate detection are present. |
| AES encryption at rest for stored value and audit detail | PASS (static) | TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/stored-value/stored-value.service.ts:2,13-14,35,42-43,85-86; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/admin/audit.service.ts:2,37-39,84 | Encrypted persistence and decrypt-on-read patterns exist. |
| TLS required even on LAN | FAIL | TASK-15/prompt.md:5; TASK-15/repo/README.md:161-166; TASK-15/repo/docs/acceptance-checklist.md:20 | Prompt requires TLS on LAN, but implementation/docs explicitly declare HTTP-only with optional reverse proxy. |
| API signing for privileged integrations | FAIL | TASK-15/prompt.md:5; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/api-signing.middleware.ts:14-48; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/classroom/classroom.routes.ts:18 | Middleware exists but is not wired into route chains; classroom route comment explicitly says HMAC is future/production note. || Observability routes usable under authenticated admin flow | FAIL | TASK-15/repo/backend/src/routes/index.ts:85-88; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/metrics.routes.ts:13-22; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/log.routes.ts:8; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:71-73 | Observability routes use requirePermission but do not set req.user via authenticate, so they return 401 by middleware logic. |
| Idempotency consistency on create/update operations | FAIL | TASK-15/prompt.md:5; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/fulfillment/fulfillment.routes.ts:3,15; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/idempotency.middleware.ts:14; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/warehouse/warehouse.routes.ts:13,15; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/shipment/shipment.routes.ts:20-45 | Not consistently applied across mutating routes; fulfillment imports idempotencyMiddleware symbol that is not exported by middleware file (export is idempotency). |
| Circuit breaker requirement | PARTIAL (implementation present, wiring not evidenced) | TASK-15/repo/backend/src/lib/circuit-breaker.ts:11-45; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/shipment/carrier-sync.service.ts:103-228; TASK-15/repo/docs/acceptance-checklist.md:47 | Helper exists, but no call sites found in carrier sync path despite checklist claim of wrapping calls. |
| Scheduled backups + tested restore procedure (daily full backup, 14-day retention) | PARTIAL/FAIL | TASK-15/prompt.md:5; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/config/index.ts:67-70; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/backup.service.ts:127-135,191-204,263-264; TASK-15/repo/docs/restore.md:5-8; TASK-15/repo/README.md:172 | Daily schedule and retention are present, but backup content is manifest/row-count JSON, not full DB dump; restore test is documented manual and depends on separate mysqldump. |

## 3) Prioritized Findings (Security-First)

### P0-1: Prompt-level transport security mismatch (TLS required vs HTTP-only build)
- Why high risk: direct violation of explicit security requirement.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/prompt.md:5 (requires TLS even on LAN)
  - TASK-15/repo/README.md:161-166 (HTTP-only, TLS optional via external reverse proxy)
  - TASK-15/repo/docs/acceptance-checklist.md:20 (declares TLS not required)

### P0-2: API signing requirement not enforced on privileged ingress
- Why high risk: privileged integration endpoints are not protected by HMAC signing as required.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/prompt.md:5 (API signing required)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/api-signing.middleware.ts:14-48 (middleware exists)
  - Only middleware definition found; no invocation in route chain (search result: single occurrence at definition)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/classroom/classroom.routes.ts:18 (comment indicates HMAC is not active path)

### P0-3: Observability routes mounted without authentication middleware
- Why high risk: required admin observability functions are effectively inaccessible/incorrectly wired.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/routes/index.ts:85-88 (mounted under /api)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/metrics.routes.ts:13-22 and log/threshold/backup route files (permission checks only)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:71-73 (requirePermission returns 401 when req.user missing)

### P1-1: Idempotency requirement is not consistently implemented, and one route has symbol mismatch
- Why high risk: retry safety for mutating operations is inconsistent and potentially broken in fulfillment route.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/prompt.md:5 (consistent idempotency expected)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/fulfillment/fulfillment.routes.ts:3,15 (imports/uses idempotencyMiddleware)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/idempotency.middleware.ts:14 (exports idempotency)
  - Multiple mutating route files without idempotency usage, e.g. warehouse/carrier/shipment/stored-value routes.### P1-2: Backup/restore acceptance criterion is only partially met
- Why high risk: prompt requires daily full backup with tested restore, but implementation is manifest verification and manual restore guidance.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/backup.service.ts:127-135 (writes manifest JSON)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/backup.service.ts:193-204 (verify checks manifest structure)
  - TASK-15/repo/docs/restore.md:5-8 (manifests are not full dumps)
  - TASK-15/repo/README.md:172 (manifest-based backup statement)

### P1-3: Test strategy claims exceed actual static test behavior
- Why medium-high risk: false confidence on security and business-path correctness.
- Evidence:
  - Claimed broad API coverage: TASK-15/repo/docs/test-plan.md:64-71
  - Actual API suite overwhelmingly 401-focused (104 occurrences of expect(status).toBe(401)) in API_tests/routes/*.test.ts
  - Success-path 200 assertions appear only in health tests: TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/health.test.ts:19,26
  - Minimal explicit 403 verification and no API-signing tests detected.

## 4) Mandatory Static Test Coverage Assessment

| Requirement / Risk Point | Expected Coverage | Observed Tests | Coverage Judgment | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Health and base API availability | Positive path (200) + error shape | Present | SUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/health.test.ts:19,26,31-44 |
| Authn guard behavior | Missing/malformed/invalid token rejection | Present | SUFFICIENT (guard-only) | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/auth.test.ts:53-63,73-75 |
| Successful authenticated business flows | 2xx flows with valid token and persisted effects | Largely absent | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/docs/test-plan.md:64-71 vs only 200s at health.test.ts:19,26 |
| Role-based authorization (403 paths) | Explicit forbidden-role checks | Sparse | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/auth.test.ts:98-105; TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/observability.test.ts:194 |
| Object-level authorization and data isolation | Cross-user/cross-role ownership denial checks | Not found | MISSING | No ownership-focused API assertions found in API_tests/routes/*.test.ts |
| Idempotency replay semantics | Same key replay returns cached response and no duplicate side effect | Not validated end-to-end | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/auth.test.ts:96-105; TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/jobs.test.ts:50-57 |
| API signing enforcement | Signed/unsigned request acceptance/rejection for privileged routes | Not found | MISSING | No x-api-key/x-signature tests in API_tests or backend/unit_tests |
| Upload safeguards (mime, size, dedup) | Unit + API checks including positive and negative paths | Unit-focused; API mostly auth-guarded | PARTIAL | TASK-15/repo/backend/unit_tests/shipment.test.ts:124-163; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/after-sales/evidence.service.ts:104-166 |
| Compensation rules/caps | Rule correctness and boundaries | Present (logic-level) | PARTIAL | TASK-15/repo/backend/unit_tests/shipment.test.ts:369-429 |
| Observability and backup behavior | Positive admin-path retrieval/create/verify and permission splits | Mostly unauthorized path checks | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/observability.test.ts:23-158 |
| TLS and circuit-breaker behavior | Integration/runtime tests proving enforcement | Not found | MISSING | No runtime security transport/circuit-breaker tests detected |

### Unit test conclusion
- Strength: good pure-logic coverage for crypto, masking, pricing math, and state transition helpers.
- Limitation: many tests validate local helper logic rather than full service + persistence + middleware interaction.

### API test conclusion
- Strength: consistent error-shape and unauthorized-guard assertions.
- Limitation: business-positive paths, role-forbidden paths, object-level controls, and integration-signing controls are under-covered or absent.### Runtime logs conclusion
- Unconfirmed due execution constraint (Docker commands not run). No runtime log evidence was collected in this audit pass.

## 5) Reproducible Runtime Validation Commands (Not Executed in This Pass)

1. Start full stack:
   - cd TASK-15/repo
   - docker compose up
2. Run full test runner:
   - cd TASK-15/repo
   - bash run_tests.sh
3. Run API tests only:
   - cd TASK-15/repo
   - docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm api-test-runner
4. Validate observability route auth wiring after fixes:
   - Obtain valid admin JWT via POST /api/auth/login
   - Call GET /api/metrics and GET /api/logs with Authorization header, expect 200
5. Validate API signing after wiring:
   - Call privileged endpoint without signing headers, expect 401
   - Call same endpoint with valid HMAC headers, expect 2xx/4xx domain response (not auth failure)
6. Validate idempotency replay behavior:
   - Send duplicate POST with same X-Idempotency-Key, verify second response is replayed and no extra write

## 6) Final Acceptance Judgment

Overall result: FAIL (not ready for final acceptance).

Reason: multiple security-critical prompt requirements are not met or not enforceably wired (TLS-on-LAN, API signing enforcement, observability auth chain), plus significant idempotency consistency and test-coverage gaps that prevent high-confidence acceptance.

### P0-3: Observability routes mounted without authentication middleware
- Why high risk: required admin observability functions are effectively inaccessible/incorrectly wired.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/routes/index.ts:85-88 (mounted under /api)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/metrics.routes.ts:13-22 and log/threshold/backup route files (permission checks only)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:71-73 (requirePermission returns 401 when req.user missing)

### P1-1: Idempotency requirement is not consistently implemented, and one route has symbol mismatch
- Why high risk: retry safety for mutating operations is inconsistent and potentially broken in fulfillment route.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/prompt.md:5 (consistent idempotency expected)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/fulfillment/fulfillment.routes.ts:3,15 (imports/uses idempotencyMiddleware)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/middleware/idempotency.middleware.ts:14 (exports idempotency)
  - Multiple mutating route files without idempotency usage, e.g. warehouse/carrier/shipment/stored-value routes.### P1-2: Backup/restore acceptance criterion is only partially met
- Why high risk: prompt requires daily full backup with tested restore, but implementation is manifest verification and manual restore guidance.
- Evidence:
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/backup.service.ts:127-135 (writes manifest JSON)
  - TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/observability/backup.service.ts:193-204 (verify checks manifest structure)
  - TASK-15/repo/docs/restore.md:5-8 (manifests are not full dumps)
  - TASK-15/repo/README.md:172 (manifest-based backup statement)

### P1-3: Test strategy claims exceed actual static test behavior
- Why medium-high risk: false confidence on security and business-path correctness.
- Evidence:
  - Claimed broad API coverage: TASK-15/repo/docs/test-plan.md:64-71
  - Actual API suite overwhelmingly 401-focused (104 occurrences of expect(status).toBe(401)) in API_tests/routes/*.test.ts
  - Success-path 200 assertions appear only in health tests: TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/health.test.ts:19,26
  - Minimal explicit 403 verification and no API-signing tests detected.

## 4) Mandatory Static Test Coverage Assessment

| Requirement / Risk Point | Expected Coverage | Observed Tests | Coverage Judgment | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Health and base API availability | Positive path (200) + error shape | Present | SUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/health.test.ts:19,26,31-44 |
| Authn guard behavior | Missing/malformed/invalid token rejection | Present | SUFFICIENT (guard-only) | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/auth.test.ts:53-63,73-75 |
| Successful authenticated business flows | 2xx flows with valid token and persisted effects | Largely absent | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/docs/test-plan.md:64-71 vs only 200s at health.test.ts:19,26 |
| Role-based authorization (403 paths) | Explicit forbidden-role checks | Sparse | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/auth.test.ts:98-105; TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/observability.test.ts:194 |
| Object-level authorization and data isolation | Cross-user/cross-role ownership denial checks | Not found | MISSING | No ownership-focused API assertions found in API_tests/routes/*.test.ts |
| Idempotency replay semantics | Same key replay returns cached response and no duplicate side effect | Not validated end-to-end | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/auth.test.ts:96-105; TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/jobs.test.ts:50-57 |
| API signing enforcement | Signed/unsigned request acceptance/rejection for privileged routes | Not found | MISSING | No x-api-key/x-signature tests in API_tests or backend/unit_tests |
| Upload safeguards (mime, size, dedup) | Unit + API checks including positive and negative paths | Unit-focused; API mostly auth-guarded | PARTIAL | TASK-15/repo/backend/unit_tests/shipment.test.ts:124-163; TASK-15/repo/backend/src/modules/after-sales/evidence.service.ts:104-166 |
| Compensation rules/caps | Rule correctness and boundaries | Present (logic-level) | PARTIAL | TASK-15/repo/backend/unit_tests/shipment.test.ts:369-429 |
| Observability and backup behavior | Positive admin-path retrieval/create/verify and permission splits | Mostly unauthorized path checks | INSUFFICIENT | TASK-15/repo/API_tests/routes/observability.test.ts:23-158 |
| TLS and circuit-breaker behavior | Integration/runtime tests proving enforcement | Not found | MISSING | No runtime security transport/circuit-breaker tests detected |

### Unit test conclusion
- Strength: good pure-logic coverage for crypto, masking, pricing math, and state transition helpers.
- Limitation: many tests validate local helper logic rather than full service + persistence + middleware interaction.

### API test conclusion
- Strength: consistent error-shape and unauthorized-guard assertions.
- Limitation: business-positive paths, role-forbidden paths, object-level controls, and integration-signing controls are under-covered or absent.### Runtime logs conclusion
- Unconfirmed due execution constraint (Docker commands not run). No runtime log evidence was collected in this audit pass.

## 5) Reproducible Runtime Validation Commands (Not Executed in This Pass)

1. Start full stack:
   - cd TASK-15/repo
   - docker compose up
2. Run full test runner:
   - cd TASK-15/repo
   - bash run_tests.sh
3. Run API tests only:
   - cd TASK-15/repo
   - docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm api-test-runner
4. Validate observability route auth wiring after fixes:
   - Obtain valid admin JWT via POST /api/auth/login
   - Call GET /api/metrics and GET /api/logs with Authorization header, expect 200
5. Validate API signing after wiring:
   - Call privileged endpoint without signing headers, expect 401
   - Call same endpoint with valid HMAC headers, expect 2xx/4xx domain response (not auth failure)
6. Validate idempotency replay behavior:
   - Send duplicate POST with same X-Idempotency-Key, verify second response is replayed and no extra write

## 6) Final Acceptance Judgment

Overall result: FAIL (not ready for final acceptance).

Reason: multiple security-critical prompt requirements are not met or not enforceably wired (TLS-on-LAN, API signing enforcement, observability auth chain), plus significant idempotency consistency and test-coverage gaps that prevent high-confidence acceptance.

file.content:
version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations Platform
# Startup: docker compose up  (from repo/ directory)
# All services communicate over campusops-net bridge.
# Ports exposed to host: frontend :80, backend :3000.
# Frontend nginx proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000.
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  db-data:
  redis-data:
  storage-data:
  logs-data:
  backups-data:

secrets:
  encryption_key:
    file: ./secrets/encryption_key.txt
  db_password:
    file: ./secrets/db_password.txt
  jwt_secret:
    file: ./secrets/jwt_secret.txt

services:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # MySQL 8 â€” primary relational datastore
  # Internal port: 3306
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    container_name: campusops-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops
      MYSQL_USER: campusops
      MYSQL_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - db-data:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./backend/database/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1 -u campusops -p$$(cat /run/secrets/db_password) --silent"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 40s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Redis 7 â€” BullMQ queue broker, rate limiting, idempotency
  # Internal port: 6379
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: campusops-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Backend â€” Express + TypeScript + Socket.IO + BullMQ
  # Exposes port 3000 on host for direct LAN access / debugging
  # DATABASE_URL assembled from parts by docker-entrypoint.sh
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PORT: "3000"
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /data/storage
      LOG_PATH: /logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /backups
      TZ: America/New_York
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - storage-data:/data/storage
      - logs-data:/logs
      - backups-data:/backups
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 6
      start_period: 45s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Frontend â€” Vue 3 production build served by Nginx
  # Exposes port 80; proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy

version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps â€” Test override for API functional tests
# Usage: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.yml \
#          run --rm api-test-runner
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:

services:
  db:
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops_test

  backend:
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: test
      DB_NAME: campusops_test

  # Test runner â€” runs Supertest suite then exits
  api-test-runner:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-api-tests
    entrypoint: ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
    command: ["npx", "jest", "--testPathPattern=API_tests", "--forceExit", "--verbose"]
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: test
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops_test
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /tmp/test-storage
      LOG_PATH: /tmp/test-logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /tmp/test-backups
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - ./API_tests:/app/API_tests:ro
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net

7	  updateStatusHandler,
8	  triggerSyncHandler,
9	} from './shipment.controller';

11	const router = Router();
12	
13	router.get(
14	  '/',
15	  authenticate,
16	  requirePermission('shipment:read'),
17	  listShipmentsHandler,
18	);
19	
20	router.post(
21	  '/',
22	  authenticate,
23	  requirePermission('shipment:write'),
24	  createShipmentHandler,
25	);
26	
27	router.get(
28	  '/:id',
29	  authenticate,
30	  requirePermission('shipment:read'),
31	  getShipmentHandler,
32	);
33	
34	router.patch(
35	  '/:id/status',
36	  authenticate,
37	  requirePermission('shipment:intervene'),
38	  updateStatusHandler,
39	);
40	
41	router.post(
42	  '/sync/:carrierId',
43	  authenticate,
44	  requirePermission('shipment:intervene'),
45	  triggerSyncHandler,
46	);
47	
48	export default router;

file.content:
FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY tsconfig.json ./
COPY prisma ./prisma
COPY src ./src

# Generate Prisma client
RUN npx prisma generate

# Compile TypeScript
RUN npm run build

# ============================================================
FROM node:18-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

RUN apk add --no-cache openssl wget

# Copy built app and production deps
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
COPY package*.json ./

# Copy entrypoint (handles secret â†’ DATABASE_URL)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Create required runtime directories
RUN mkdir -p /data/storage /logs /backups

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=5s --retries=5 --start-period=45s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:1.25-alpine AS production
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

EXPOSE 80

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:80/ || exit 1

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
# ============================================================
# Backend entrypoint â€” assembles DATABASE_URL from Docker
# secrets and component env vars, then runs migrations and
# starts the application.
# ============================================================
set -e

SECRET_PATH="/run/secrets/db_password"

if [ -f "$SECRET_PATH" ]; then
  DB_PASSWORD=$(cat "$SECRET_PATH")
else
  # Fallback for local dev without Docker secrets
  DB_PASSWORD="${DB_PASSWORD:-campusops_dev}"
fi

export DATABASE_URL="mysql://${DB_USER:-campusops}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST:-db}:${DB_PORT:-3306}/${DB_NAME:-campusops}"

echo "[entrypoint] Database host: ${DB_HOST:-db}:${DB_PORT:-3306}/${DB_NAME:-campusops}"
echo "[entrypoint] Syncing database schema (prisma db push)..."
npx prisma db push --schema ./prisma/schema.prisma --accept-data-loss

echo "[entrypoint] Seeding default data if needed..."
node dist/database/seeders/seed.js || echo "[entrypoint] Seed skipped (already seeded or error tolerated)"

echo "[entrypoint] Starting application..."
exec "$@"

import type { UserRole } from '../types';

// ============================================================
// Permission matrix â€” single source of truth for all RBAC
// checks in the backend.
// ============================================================

export type Permission =
  // Auth & users
  | 'users:read'
  | 'users:create'
  | 'users:update'
  | 'users:delete'
  | 'users:change-role'
  // Audit
  | 'audit:read'
  | 'audit:reveal-pii'
  // Settings & keys
  | 'settings:read'
  | 'settings:update'
  | 'integration-keys:manage'
  // Backup
  | 'backup:read'
  | 'backup:manage'
  // Master data
  | 'master-data:read'
  | 'master-data:write'
  | 'students:read'
  | 'students:write'
  | 'students:import'
  // Classroom
  | 'classroom:read'
  | 'classroom:manage'
  | 'anomaly:acknowledge'
  | 'anomaly:assign'
  | 'anomaly:resolve'
  // Parking
  | 'parking:read'
  | 'parking:manage'
  | 'parking-alert:claim'
  | 'parking-alert:close'
  // Warehouse & logistics
  | 'warehouse:read'
  | 'warehouse:write'
  | 'carrier:read'
  | 'carrier:write'
  | 'delivery-zone:read'
  | 'delivery-zone:write'
  | 'shipping-template:read'
  | 'shipping-template:write'
  // Membership
  | 'membership:read'
  | 'membership:write'
  | 'coupon:read'
  | 'coupon:write'
  // Fulfillment
  | 'fulfillment:read'
  | 'fulfillment:create'
  | 'fulfillment:manage'
  // Shipments
  | 'shipment:read'
  | 'shipment:write'
  | 'shipment:intervene'
  // After-sales
  | 'after-sales:read'
  | 'after-sales:create'
  | 'after-sales:manage'
  // Compensation
  | 'compensation:suggest'
  | 'compensation:approve-limited'   // up to $25
  | 'compensation:approve-full'      // up to cap
  | 'compensation:approve-override'  // above cap
  // Stored value
  | 'stored-value:read'
  | 'stored-value:topup'
  | 'stored-value:spend'
  // Files
  | 'files:upload'
  | 'files:delete'
  // Jobs & observability
  | 'jobs:read'
  | 'jobs:manage'
  | 'metrics:read'
  | 'logs:read'
  | 'alerts:read'
  | 'alerts:manage';

const PERMISSION_MATRIX: Record<Permission, UserRole[]> = {
  // ---- Auth & users (admin only) ----
  'users:read':              ['administrator'],
  'users:create':            ['administrator'],
  'users:update':            ['administrator'],
  'users:delete':            ['administrator'],
  'users:change-role':       ['administrator'],

  // ---- Audit ----
  'audit:read':              ['administrator', 'auditor'],
  'audit:reveal-pii':        ['administrator'],

  // ---- Settings & keys ----
  'settings:read':           ['administrator'],
  'settings:update':         ['administrator'],
  'integration-keys:manage': ['administrator'],

  // ---- Backup ----
  'backup:read':             ['administrator'],
  'backup:manage':           ['administrator'],

  // ---- Master data ----
  'master-data:read':        ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'classroom_supervisor', 'customer_service_agent', 'auditor'],
  'master-data:write':       ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'students:read':           ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'classroom_supervisor', 'customer_service_agent', 'auditor'],
  'students:write':          ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'students:import':         ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],

  // ---- Classroom ----
  'classroom:read':          ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'classroom:manage':        ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'anomaly:acknowledge':     ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'anomaly:assign':          ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'anomaly:resolve':         ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],

  // ---- Parking ----
  'parking:read':            ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'parking:manage':          ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'parking-alert:claim':     ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],
  'parking-alert:close':     ['administrator', 'classroom_supervisor'],

  // ---- Warehouse & logistics ----
  'warehouse:read':          ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'warehouse:write':         ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'carrier:read':            ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'carrier:write':           ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'delivery-zone:read':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'delivery-zone:write':     ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'shipping-template:read':  ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'shipping-template:write': ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],

  // ---- Membership ----
  'membership:read':         ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'membership:write':        ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'coupon:read':             ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'coupon:write':            ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],

  // ---- Fulfillment ----
  'fulfillment:read':        ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent', 'auditor'],
  'fulfillment:create':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'fulfillment:manage':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],

  // ---- Shipments ----
  'shipment:read':           ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent', 'auditor'],
  'shipment:write':          ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'shipment:intervene':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],

  // ---- After-sales ----
  'after-sales:read':        ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent', 'auditor'],
  'after-sales:create':      ['administrator', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'after-sales:manage':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],

  // ---- Compensation ----
  'compensation:suggest':          ['administrator', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'compensation:approve-limited':  ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'compensation:approve-full':     ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'compensation:approve-override': ['administrator'],

  // ---- Stored value ----
  'stored-value:read':       ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'stored-value:topup':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'stored-value:spend':      ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],

  // ---- Files ----
  'files:upload':            ['administrator', 'operations_manager', 'customer_service_agent'],
  'files:delete':            ['administrator'],

  // ---- Jobs & observability ----
  'jobs:read':               ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'jobs:manage':             ['administrator'],
  'metrics:read':            ['administrator'],
  'logs:read':               ['administrator'],
  'alerts:read':             ['administrator', 'operations_manager'],
  'alerts:manage':           ['administrator'],
};

export function can(role: UserRole, permission: Permission): boolean {
  return PERMISSION_MATRIX[permission]?.includes(role) ?? false;
}

export function getRolePermissions(role: UserRole): Permission[] {
  return (Object.entries(PERMISSION_MATRIX) as [Permission, UserRole[]][])
    .filter(([, roles]) => roles.includes(role))
    .map(([perm]) => perm);
}

export const ROLE_LABELS: Record<UserRole, string> = {
  administrator:           'Administrator',
  operations_manager:      'Operations Manager',
  classroom_supervisor:    'Classroom Supervisor',
  customer_service_agent:  'Customer Service Agent',
  auditor:                 'Auditor',
};

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ============================================================
# run_tests.sh â€” CampusOps Test Runner
# Runs unit tests (Vitest) and API tests (Supertest/Jest)
# inside Docker containers and prints a pass/fail summary.
# ============================================================
set -euo pipefail

PASS=0
FAIL=0
SUMMARY=()

print_header() {
  echo ""
  echo "========================================"
  echo "  $1"
  echo "========================================"
}

run_suite() {
  local name="$1"
  local command="$2"
  print_header "$name"
  if eval "$command"; then
    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
    SUMMARY+=("  [PASS] $name")
  else
    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
    SUMMARY+=("  [FAIL] $name")
  fi
}

# Unit tests (Vitest â€” runs inside backend container)
run_suite "Unit Tests (Vitest)" \
  "docker compose run --rm --no-deps backend npx vitest run --reporter verbose unit_tests"

# API functional tests (Supertest/Jest â€” requires db + redis + backend)
run_suite "API Functional Tests (Supertest)" \
  "docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm api-test-runner"

# Print summary
echo ""
echo "========================================"
echo "  TEST SUMMARY"
echo "========================================"
for line in "${SUMMARY[@]}"; do
  echo "$line"
done
echo ""
echo "  Suites passed: $PASS"
echo "  Suites failed: $FAIL"
echo "========================================"

if [[ $FAIL -gt 0 ]]; then
  exit 1
fi
exit 0

content.text:
### Fix 7: Add TLS support for LAN transport security

I need to: create a self-signed cert generation script, create an nginx config with HTTPS, update the frontend Dockerfile, and update docker-compose.

First, let me create the TLS cert generation script:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ============================================================
# Generate self-signed TLS certificates for CampusOps LAN
# Run once before first docker compose up:
#   bash secrets/generate-certs.sh
# ============================================================
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
CERT_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR"

# Skip if certs already exist
if [ -f "$CERT_DIR/tls.crt" ] && [ -f "$CERT_DIR/tls.key" ]; then
  echo "[certs] TLS certificates already exist, skipping generation."
  exit 0
fi

echo "[certs] Generating self-signed TLS certificate for CampusOps LAN..."

openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 3650 \
  -newkey rsa:2048 \
  -keyout "$CERT_DIR/tls.key" \
  -out "$CERT_DIR/tls.crt" \
  -subj "/C=US/ST=Local/L=Campus/O=CampusOps/CN=campusops.local" \
  -addext "subjectAltName=DNS:campusops.local,DNS:localhost,IP:127.0.0.1"

echo "[certs] Certificate generated:"
echo "  Key:  $CERT_DIR/tls.key"
echo "  Cert: $CERT_DIR/tls.crt"
echo "[certs] Valid for 10 years. For production, replace with CA-signed certificates."

version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations Platform
# Startup: docker compose up  (from repo/ directory)
# All services communicate over campusops-net bridge.
# Ports exposed to host: frontend :80, backend :3000.
# Frontend nginx proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000.
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  db-data:
  redis-data:
  storage-data:
  logs-data:
  backups-data:

secrets:
  encryption_key:
    file: ./secrets/encryption_key.txt
  db_password:
    file: ./secrets/db_password.txt
  jwt_secret:
    file: ./secrets/jwt_secret.txt

services:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # MySQL 8 â€” primary relational datastore
  # Internal port: 3306
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    container_name: campusops-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops
      MYSQL_USER: campusops
      MYSQL_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - db-data:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./backend/database/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1 -u campusops -p$$(cat /run/secrets/db_password) --silent"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 40s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Redis 7 â€” BullMQ queue broker, rate limiting, idempotency
  # Internal port: 6379
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: campusops-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Backend â€” Express + TypeScript + Socket.IO + BullMQ
  # Exposes port 3000 on host for direct LAN access / debugging
  # DATABASE_URL assembled from parts by docker-entrypoint.sh
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PORT: "3000"
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /data/storage
      LOG_PATH: /logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /backups
      TZ: America/New_York
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - storage-data:/data/storage
      - logs-data:/logs
      - backups-data:/backups
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 6
      start_period: 45s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Frontend â€” Vue 3 production build served by Nginx
  # Exposes port 80; proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY tsconfig.json ./
COPY prisma ./prisma
COPY src ./src

# Generate Prisma client
RUN npx prisma generate

# Compile TypeScript
RUN npm run build

# ============================================================
FROM node:18-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

RUN apk add --no-cache openssl wget

# Copy built app and production deps
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
COPY package*.json ./

# Copy entrypoint (handles secret â†’ DATABASE_URL)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Create required runtime directories
RUN mkdir -p /data/storage /logs /backups

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=5s --retries=5 --start-period=45s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]

file.content:
FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY tsconfig.json ./
COPY prisma ./prisma
COPY src ./src

# Generate Prisma client
RUN npx prisma generate

# Compile TypeScript
RUN npm run build

# ============================================================
FROM node:18-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

RUN apk add --no-cache openssl wget mysql-client

# Copy built app and production deps
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
COPY package*.json ./

# Copy entrypoint (handles secret â†’ DATABASE_URL)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Create required runtime directories
RUN mkdir -p /data/storage /logs /backups

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=5s --retries=5 --start-period=45s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]

content.text: The apk error might be a transient Alpine repo issue. Let me add `--allow-untrusted` or update the package index first:

input.old_string:
# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl
input.new_string:
# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl || \
    (apk add --no-cache --repository=https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.18/main python3 make g++ openssl)

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

COPY package*.json ./

COPY prisma ./prisma
COPY src ./src

# Generate Prisma client

# Compile TypeScript
RUN npm run build

# ============================================================
FROM node:18-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

RUN apk add --no-cache openssl wget mysql-client

# Copy built app and production deps
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
COPY package*.json ./

# Copy entrypoint (handles secret â†’ DATABASE_URL)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Create required runtime directories
RUN mkdir -p /data/storage /logs /backups

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=5s --retries=5 --start-period=45s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]

message.content: please remove anything related with nginx we don't need it the config and other realted things with nginc in the docker and docker ompose also please make sure everything realted with docker is working in a good way and the tests should be runnable and strong have coverage

content.text: Let me clean up all nginx/TLS-related changes and fix the Docker setup.

content.content:
1	FROM node:18-alpine AS builder
2	
3	WORKDIR /app
4	
5	COPY package.json ./
6	RUN npm install
7	
8	COPY . .
9	RUN npm run build
10	
11	FROM nginx:1.25-alpine AS production
12	
13	# Remove default nginx config
14	RUN rm -f /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
15	
16	# Copy custom nginx config with TLS support
17	COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
18	COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
19	
20	# Create directory for TLS certificates (mounted at runtime)
21	RUN mkdir -p /etc/nginx/certs
22	
23	EXPOSE 80 443
24	
25	HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
26	  CMD wget --no-check-certificate -qO- https://localhost:443/ || wget -qO- http://localhost:80/ || exit 1
27

file.content:
FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY package.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:1.25-alpine AS production

# Remove default nginx config
RUN rm -f /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf

# Copy custom nginx config with TLS support
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

# Create directory for TLS certificates (mounted at runtime)
RUN mkdir -p /etc/nginx/certs

EXPOSE 80 443

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-check-certificate -qO- https://localhost:443/ || wget -qO- http://localhost:80/ || exit 1

file.content:
version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations Platform
# Startup: docker compose up  (from repo/ directory)
# All services communicate over campusops-net bridge.
# Ports exposed to host: frontend :80, backend :3000.
# Frontend nginx proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000.
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  db-data:
  redis-data:
  storage-data:
  logs-data:
  backups-data:

secrets:
  encryption_key:
    file: ./secrets/encryption_key.txt
  db_password:
    file: ./secrets/db_password.txt
  jwt_secret:
    file: ./secrets/jwt_secret.txt
  tls_cert:
    file: ./secrets/tls.crt
  tls_key:
    file: ./secrets/tls.key

services:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # MySQL 8 â€” primary relational datastore
  # Internal port: 3306
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    container_name: campusops-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops
      MYSQL_USER: campusops
      MYSQL_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - db-data:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./backend/database/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1 -u campusops -p$$(cat /run/secrets/db_password) --silent"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 40s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Redis 7 â€” BullMQ queue broker, rate limiting, idempotency
  # Internal port: 6379
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: campusops-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Backend â€” Express + TypeScript + Socket.IO + BullMQ
  # Exposes port 3000 on host for direct LAN access / debugging
  # DATABASE_URL assembled from parts by docker-entrypoint.sh
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PORT: "3000"
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /data/storage
      LOG_PATH: /logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /backups
      TZ: America/New_York
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - storage-data:/data/storage
      - logs-data:/logs
      - backups-data:/backups
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 6
      start_period: 45s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Frontend â€” Vue 3 production build served by Nginx
  # Exposes port 80; proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./secrets/tls.crt:/etc/nginx/certs/tls.crt:ro
      - ./secrets/tls.key:/etc/nginx/certs/tls.key:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy

file.content:
FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl || \
    (apk add --no-cache --repository=https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.18/main python3 make g++ openssl)

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY tsconfig.json ./
COPY prisma ./prisma
COPY src ./src

# Generate Prisma client
RUN npx prisma generate

# Compile TypeScript
RUN npm run build

# ============================================================
FROM node:18-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

RUN apk add --no-cache openssl wget mysql-client

# Copy built app and production deps
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
COPY package*.json ./

# Copy entrypoint (handles secret â†’ DATABASE_URL)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Create required runtime directories
RUN mkdir -p /data/storage /logs /backups

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=5s --retries=5 --start-period=45s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]

file.content:
version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps â€” Test override for API functional tests
# Usage: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.yml \
#          run --rm api-test-runner
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:

services:
  db:
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops_test

  backend:
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: test
      DB_NAME: campusops_test

  # Test runner â€” runs Supertest suite then exits
  api-test-runner:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-api-tests
    entrypoint: ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
    command: ["npx", "jest", "--testPathPattern=API_tests", "--forceExit", "--verbose"]
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: test
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops_test
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /tmp/test-storage
      LOG_PATH: /tmp/test-logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /tmp/test-backups
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - ./API_tests:/app/API_tests:ro
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net

      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops
      MYSQL_USER: campusops
      MYSQL_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - db-data:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./backend/database/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1 -u campusops -p$$(cat /run/secrets/db_password) --silent"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 40s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Redis 7 â€” BullMQ queue broker, rate limiting, idempotency
  # Internal port: 6379
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: campusops-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Backend â€” Express + TypeScript + Socket.IO + BullMQ
  # Exposes port 3000 on host for direct LAN access / debugging
  # DATABASE_URL assembled from parts by docker-entrypoint.sh
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PORT: "3000"
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /data/storage
      LOG_PATH: /logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /backups
      TZ: America/New_York
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - storage-data:/data/storage
      - logs-data:/logs
      - backups-data:/backups
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 6
      start_period: 45s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Frontend â€” Vue 3 production build served by Nginx
  # Exposes port 80; proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./secrets/tls.crt:/etc/nginx/certs/tls.crt:ro
      - ./secrets/tls.key:/etc/nginx/certs/tls.key:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy

file.content:
version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations Platform
# Startup: docker compose up  (from repo/ directory)
# All services communicate over campusops-net bridge.
# Ports exposed to host: frontend :80, backend :3000.
# Frontend nginx proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000.
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  db-data:
  redis-data:
  storage-data:
  logs-data:
  backups-data:

secrets:
  encryption_key:
    file: ./secrets/encryption_key.txt
  db_password:
    file: ./secrets/db_password.txt
  jwt_secret:
    file: ./secrets/jwt_secret.txt

services:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # MySQL 8 â€” primary relational datastore
  # Internal port: 3306
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  db:
    image: mysql:8.0
    container_name: campusops-db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops
      MYSQL_USER: campusops
      MYSQL_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - db-data:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./backend/database/init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1 -u campusops -p$$(cat /run/secrets/db_password) --silent"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 12
      start_period: 40s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Redis 7 â€” BullMQ queue broker, rate limiting, idempotency
  # Internal port: 6379
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: campusops-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Backend â€” Express + TypeScript + Socket.IO + BullMQ
  # Exposes port 3000 on host for direct LAN access / debugging
  # DATABASE_URL assembled from parts by docker-entrypoint.sh
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  backend:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PORT: "3000"
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /data/storage
      LOG_PATH: /logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /backups
      TZ: America/New_York
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - storage-data:/data/storage
      - logs-data:/logs
      - backups-data:/backups
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 6
      start_period: 45s

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Frontend â€” Vue 3 production build served by Nginx
  # Exposes port 80; proxies /api/* and /socket.io/* â†’ backend:3000
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  frontend:
    build:
      context: ./frontend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
    networks:
      - campusops-net
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy

content.text:
Good, docker-compose.yml is clean. Now let me check the test runner can find and run the tests. The test runner mounts `./API_tests:/app/API_tests:ro` and runs `jest --testPathPattern=API_tests`. The tests import from `../../src/app` which means they need the backend source compiled. Let me check the test runner uses the backend Dockerfile, so it has the built app. But the tests also import helpers from `../helpers/setup` â€” let me make sure the paths work.

The test runner builds from `./backend` context but mounts `./API_tests` at `/app/API_tests`. The tests import `../../src/app` from `API_tests/helpers/setup.ts` â€” that resolves to `/app/src/app` but the built code is at `/app/dist`. The tests need to be compiled too.

Let me check if there's a jest config that handles TypeScript:

content.text:
Good â€” `ts-jest` handles the TypeScript compilation for tests. The `testMatch` is `**/API_tests/**/*.test.ts`. But the test runner mounts tests at `/app/API_tests` â€” the jest config is in `/app/jest.config.js` and the tests import from `../../src/app` which from `/app/API_tests/helpers/setup.ts` resolves to `/app/src/app`. Since ts-jest compiles on the fly, and the source is at `/app/src`, this should work. But wait â€” the Docker image only has `/app/dist`, not `/app/src`. The test runner needs the source code too.

Let me check the test runner more carefully:

content.content: File unchanged since last read. The content from the earlier Read tool_result in this conversation is still current â€” refer to that instead of re-reading.

content.text:
The test runner uses the same `Dockerfile` which only copies `dist`, `node_modules`, and `prisma` to production stage â€” no `src/`. The tests need source files for `ts-jest`. I need to either:
1. Mount the backend source into the test runner, or
2. Add a test-specific Dockerfile stage

Option 1 is simpler. Let me update docker-compose.test.yml:

input.content:
version: "3.9"

# ============================================================
# CampusOps â€” Test override for API functional tests
# Usage: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.yml \
#          run --rm api-test-runner
# ============================================================

networks:
  campusops-net:

services:
  db:
    environment:
      MYSQL_DATABASE: campusops_test

  backend:
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: test
      DB_NAME: campusops_test

  # Test runner â€” runs Supertest suite then exits
  api-test-runner:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: campusops-api-tests
    entrypoint: ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
    command: ["npx", "jest", "--testPathPattern=API_tests", "--forceExit", "--runInBand", "--verbose"]
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: test
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "3306"
      DB_NAME: campusops_test
      DB_USER: campusops
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      STORAGE_PATH: /tmp/test-storage
      LOG_PATH: /tmp/test-logs
      BACKUP_PATH: /tmp/test-backups
    secrets:
      - encryption_key
      - db_password
      - jwt_secret
    volumes:
      - ./API_tests:/app/API_tests:ro
      - ./backend/src:/app/src:ro
      - ./backend/jest.config.js:/app/jest.config.js:ro
      - ./backend/tsconfig.json:/app/tsconfig.json:ro
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - campusops-net

#!/usr/bin/env sh
# ============================================================
# Backend entrypoint â€” assembles DATABASE_URL from Docker
# secrets and component env vars, then runs migrations and
# starts the application.
# ============================================================
set -e

SECRET_PATH="/run/secrets/db_password"

if [ -f "$SECRET_PATH" ]; then
  DB_PASSWORD=$(cat "$SECRET_PATH")
else
  # Fallback for local dev without Docker secrets
  DB_PASSWORD="${DB_PASSWORD:-campusops_dev}"
fi

export DATABASE_URL="mysql://${DB_USER:-campusops}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST:-db}:${DB_PORT:-3306}/${DB_NAME:-campusops}"

echo "[entrypoint] Database host: ${DB_HOST:-db}:${DB_PORT:-3306}/${DB_NAME:-campusops}"
echo "[entrypoint] Syncing database schema (prisma db push)..."
npx prisma db push --schema ./prisma/schema.prisma --accept-data-loss

echo "[entrypoint] Seeding default data if needed..."
node dist/database/seeders/seed.js || echo "[entrypoint] Seed skipped (already seeded or error tolerated)"

echo "[entrypoint] Starting application..."
exec "$@"

content.text:
The entrypoint runs `prisma db push` and then `node dist/database/seeders/seed.js`. But in the test runner, `dist/database/seeders/seed.js` should exist since it uses the same Dockerfile. Good.

However there's a problem â€” the test runner runs `npx jest` which will use `ts-jest` to compile the tests, but the `src/` directory is mounted read-only. The `ts-jest` will compile on the fly in memory so that should be fine.

FROM node:18-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install OS deps needed by sharp and prisma
RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache python3 make g++ openssl

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

COPY tsconfig.json ./
COPY prisma ./prisma
COPY src ./src

# Generate Prisma client
RUN npx prisma generate

# Compile TypeScript
RUN npm run build

# ============================================================
FROM node:18-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache openssl wget mysql-client

# Copy built app and production deps
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/prisma ./prisma
COPY package*.json ./

# Copy entrypoint (handles secret â†’ DATABASE_URL)
COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh

# Create required runtime directories
RUN mkdir -p /data/storage /logs /backups

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=15s --timeout=5s --retries=5 --start-period=45s \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
